title: Melted French Manchego Tart
description: A romantic French dish featuring Manchego cheese, fresh puff pastry, and a touch of sous-vide magic, all without onions or garlic.

ingredients.name: Manchego cheese
ingredients.notes: Aged Manchego from Spain for the best flavor.

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) for a crispy pastry crust.
Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm) and trim to a circle of 12 inches (30 cm) in diameter.
Place the pastry circle on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Mix the Manchego cheese with the crumbled bacon lardons and chopped thyme in a small bowl.
Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the center of the pastry circle, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Fold the pastry border over the filling to create a crust, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the pastry with the beaten egg and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Place the tart in the oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, prepare the sous-vide water bath to 130°F (54°C).
Place the tart in the sous-vide water bath for an additional 10 minutes, or until the filling is melted and the pastry is fluffy.
Remove the tart from the water bath and let it cool for 5 minutes on a wire rack.
Serve the tart warm, garnished with whipped creme fraiche and a sprinkle of paprika.
Garnish with fresh parsley and a sprinkle of sage leaves, if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 2 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
